questions.question: If the frequency of a sound wave doubles, its wavelength in the same medium
questions.explanation: Speed of sound is constant in a medium: v = f × λ, so if f doubles, λ halves.

questions.question: In an echo, the minimum distance of a reflecting surface for a 0.2 s delay in air at 20°C is
questions.explanation: Speed of sound ≈ 343 m/s. Minimum distance = (v × t)/2 = 343 × 0.2 /2 = 34.3 ≈ 34.4 m.

questions.question: Two sounds of same loudness are produced. One has frequency 500 Hz, other 1000 Hz. Which sound has higher energy per second?
questions.explanation: Energy depends on amplitude, not frequency, for the same medium and loudness.

questions.question: A sound wave travels 1 km in air at 20°C. The time taken is approximately
questions.explanation: Speed of sound ≈ 343 m/s. Time = distance/speed = 1000/343 ≈ 2.91 s.

questions.question: A siren emits sound at 660 Hz. If it approaches a stationary observer at 30 m/s, frequency heard is (speed of sound = 330 m/s)
The fundamental frequency of a stretched string is 256 Hz. Its first overtone is
questions.explanation: First overtone = 2 × fundamental frequency = 2 × 256 = 512 Hz.

questions.question: Reverberation time can be reduced by using
questions.explanation: Soft materials absorb sound and reduce persistence (reverberation).

questions.question: Ultrasonic waves cannot be used for
questions.explanation: Musical instruments use audible frequencies, not ultrasonic.

questions.question: The speed of sound in a gas is directly proportional to
questions.explanation: v ∝ √T; speed increases with square root of absolute temperature.

questions.question: Which of the following represents compression in a sound wave?
questions.explanation: Compression is the region of high particle density/pressure in a longitudinal wave.

questions.question: In sonar, if distance to object doubles, time for echo is
questions.explanation: Time ∝ distance; doubling distance doubles time for sound to travel to object and back.

questions.question: Frequency of sound increases when
questions.explanation: Due to Doppler effect, approaching source → perceived frequency increases.

questions.question: If a string is tightened, its frequency
questions.explanation: Tension ↑ → wave velocity ↑ → frequency ↑ (f = v/2L for a string).

questions.question: The intensity of sound is proportional to
questions.explanation: Intensity ∝ square of amplitude of the wave.

questions.question: An observer hears a sound at 440 Hz. The source moves away. The observer hears
questions.explanation: Source moving away → observed frequency decreases (Doppler effect).

questions.question: A tuning fork is struck and produces 512 Hz. If another fork produces the same pitch, it must have
questions.explanation: Same pitch = same frequency regardless of amplitude.

questions.question: If sound passes from air to water, its speed
questions.explanation: Sound travels faster in denser mediums like water than in air.

questions.question: Wavelength of a 256 Hz sound in air (v = 340 m/s) is
A sound wave of wavelength 0.5 m travels with speed 343 m/s. Its frequency is
A sound wave has amplitude doubled. Its intensity increases by
questions.explanation: Intensity ∝ amplitude² → doubling amplitude quadruples intensity.

questions.question: A sound of 1000 Hz enters a room. Another sound of 1000 Hz is played. The phenomenon of loud and soft sounds occurs due to
questions.explanation: Interference of two waves of same frequency produces beats (alternating loud and soft sounds).

questions.question: The frequency range above 20 kHz is called
questions.explanation: Ultrasonic waves have frequencies > 20 kHz.

questions.question: A longitudinal wave in a spring is produced by
questions.explanation: Longitudinal waves are produced by compressing and releasing the medium in the direction of propagation.

questions.question: In a musical instrument, harmonics are produced due to
questions.explanation: Harmonics arise from superposition of multiple frequencies along with fundamental frequency.

questions.question: If speed of sound in air is 343 m/s and frequency is 512 Hz, its time period is
questions.explanation: Time period T = 1/f = 1/512 ≈ 0.00195 s.
